business.headline: Amid Hormuz closure, OPEC+ raises oil output quotas, no mention of UAE withdrawalSeven OPEC+ countries will raise oil output targets by 188,000 barrels per day in June, the third consecutive monthly increase, OPEC+ said in a statement after an online meeting. The increase is the same as that agreed for May minus the share of the United Arab Emirates, which left the group on May 1.
AI is helping you invest. But should you trust it?From picking stocks to timing trades, AI is becoming your new investment guide. The real question is, should you trust it with your money?

Mandatory but delayed: Why third-party motor insurance claims take years to settleHandcrafted ByOur Editors In the fourth story in our series Motor Mess, which looks at issues in motor insurance, we spoke to industry experts to understand why claims take years to settle.

Air India to cut international flights till July amid fuel surge, airspace curbsAir India will cut international flights from June to July as rising ATF prices, airspace restrictions, and longer routes make several operations unprofitable, CEO Campbell Wilson said.

How best to tackle oil shock? Dr Sajjid Z Chinoy respondsIn an interview with India Today, Dr Sajjid Z Chinoy, Chief India Economist at JP Morgan, discussed the global energy shock and its impact on the Indian economy following the recent hikes in commercial LPG and anticipated domestic fuel price increases.

Not complying with trade deal: Trump hikes tariffs on cars, trucks from EU to 25%The move comes months after Trump and European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen agreed to a new trade deal in July 2025, which set a 15 per cent tariff on most goods, including automobiles and auto parts.

Petrol, diesel prices may be hiked by Rs 4-5 per litre, LPG by Rs 40-50: SourcesPetrol and diesel prices may increase by Rs four to five per litre, while domestic LPG cylinder prices could rise by Rs 40-50 per cylinder, according to government sources.

Fuel, LPG prices may go up, decision likely within 5-7 days: Government sourcesPetrol, diesel prices may rise by Rs 4–5 per litre and LPG by Rs 40–50 per cylinder, following a sharp jump in global crude oil prices driven by West Asia war.

Will your minimum pension rise from Rs 1,000 to Rs 7,500? Find out who benefitsPensioners across the country may soon see a shift in their monthly income if the proposed changes go through. Here's what we know so far and who could benefit.

8th Pay Commission to visit Hyderabad, Srinagar, Ladakh: What does this mean?The 8th Pay Commission is taking its discussions beyond Delhi, with visits planned to Hyderabad, Srinagar and Ladakh. But what does this outreach mean for government employees and pensioners awaiting revisions?

Paytm sees strong user trust and high merchant stickiness post PPBL actionBrokerage firms tracking the company said the RBI action on PPBL has limited direct impact on Paytm's core business operations, given the structural separation between the listed entity and the payments bank.

Earning Rs 50,000 a month? The right way to spend, save and investManaging money at Rs 50,000 a month isn't just about covering expenses, it's about making choices that shape your financial future. But are you allocating it in the right way?

From petrol to food, why could everything get costlier togetherA mix of higher oil prices, a weaker rupee and monsoon risks is putting pressure on India's economy. The overlap could lift household costs, limit policy choices and raise stagflation concerns.

GST at record Rs 2.43 lakh crore in April, but domestic demand growth slowsA major part of April's growth came from import-related revenues. GST collected on imports rose sharply by 25.8% to Rs 57,580 crore.

Workplace injury claims jump 31%: Are businesses doing enough?On Labour Day, as conversations around worker safety and rights take centre stage, a pressing question emerges—are businesses truly doing enough to protect their workforce? A recent report suggests concerns are growing.

Even the bank didn't help me: What victims face after reporting digital fraudFor part two of our series on Banking Risks in Digital India, I spoke to victims of digital fraud to understand what they experienced after being scammed. Their stories show how the aftermath can be just as distressing as the fraud itself.

Commercial LPG up by nearly Rs 1,000: Why have prices increased?Commercial LPG cylinder prices have risen again as global oil tensions keep costs elevated. The move shields households for now but raises pressure on restaurants, hotels and small businesses.

Maharashtra Day on May 1: Is the stock market open or closed today?The BSE and NSE are closed on May 1 for Maharashtra Day across key trading segments. Commodity derivatives will reopen in the evening, while traders should account for settlement and liquidity effects.

Commercial, mini LPG prices hiked by Rs 993 and Rs 261, no change in domestic ratesCommercial LPG cylinder prices have surged by Rs 993 to Rs 3,071.50 in Delhi, while domestic LPG, petrol, diesel, and kerosene rates remain unchanged to shield consumers.

Bajaj Finance raises FD rates up to 7.40%: Should you lock in now?The company has increased rates by up to 45 basis points, with the biggest jump seen in longer tenures. This means investors looking to park money for a few years could now earn better returns.

entertainment.headline: Anil Kapoor marks 41 years of Yudh, recalls nervous dance moment with Hema Malini Anil Kapoor revisited memories of his 1985 film Yudh as it completed 41 years, reflecting on an early career phase. He said playing a double role was easy, but dancing with Hema Malini left him nervous and challenged by her screen presence.

Janhvi Kapoor shares post calling out misleading reports about her alcohol remark Janhvi Kapoor's initiative for alcohol addiction awareness issued a statement clarifying that her recent remarks on alcohol had been misinterpreted by sections of the media. The statement described the actor as a caregiver and ally associated with the cause.

Actor-producer Sudesh Kumar dies at 95 in Mumbai, prayer meet on May 4 Actor and producer Sudesh Kumar died at 95 in Mumbai after health complications. He is survived by his wife and daughter. A prayer meet is scheduled for the late artiste on May 4.

Rahul Roy urges fans not to believe fake videos seeking money in his name Rahul Roy asked viewers not to believe or engage with fake videos seeking money in his name. He clarified that he is safe, well cared for by his family and his lifestyle choices are personal.

Viral: Jaafar Jackson reacts to Bharatanatyam fusion on Michael Jackson song Jaafar Jackson reacted to a viral Indian classical fusion dance video set to a Michael Jackson song. His comment on dancer Swetha Warrier's tribute reel drew wider attention online.

Ranveer Allahbadia takes cheeky dig at past row after Kapil Sharma show. See post Ranveer Allahbadia grabbed attention with his post after The Kapil show appearance, taking a cheeky dig at India's Got Latent row while also dedicating the moment to his late grandfather.

Ssunita Ahuja takes fresh dig at Govinda's shooting incident on Laughter Chefs 3 Ssunita Ahuja joked about Govinda and his shooting incident during Laughter Chefs 3's appearance. The remark, along with ongoing rumours around their marriage, has renewed attention on the couple.

Salman Khan serves style and life lessons in viral fedora look Salman Khan grabbed attention with his black fedora look in new photos shared from his farm. His Hinglish caption, carrying a simple message about clarity and honesty, quickly went viral.

Does Pati Patni Aur Woh Do promote infidelity? Ayushmann Khurrana responds Actor Ayushmann Khurrana addressed claims that his upcoming film Pati Patni Aur Woh Do promotes infidelity at the trailer launch. He said the sequel is a family entertainer and a comedy of errors built on situational humour.

Samay Raina, Sunil Pal turn tension into viral comedy moment on Kapil Sharma show A recent episode of The Great Indian Kapil Show turned an off-screen feud into on-screen comedy. Sunil Pal and Samay Raina came face to face, and the internet can't stop talking about it.

SRK helps pregnant Deepika climb stairs on King set, fans call him gem A behind-the-scenes clip from the King set shows Shah Rukh Khan offering Deepika Padukone a hand between takes in South Africa. The moment has gone viral soon after leaked images from the film's set drew attention online.

Shakira takes over Rio: 2 million fans, fireworks and one epic show Shakira opened her free Copacabana Beach concert in Rio on a grand note. The night stood out for the huge turnout and her first live performance of Choka Choka with Anitta.

Raja Shivaji box office Day 2: Riteish's film holds firm despite slight dip Riteish Deshmukh's Raja Shivaji continued its strong box office run on Day 2 despite a slight dip. The Marathi version remained the main contributor, while the Hindi release posed a steady start.

Diljit stops Canada show, confronts pro-Khalistanis: Jinne jhande dikhane... Diljit Dosanjh paused his concert in Calgary, Canada, to address pro-Khalistani protesters waving flags during his show. The singer asked those disrupting the show to leave the act aside.

M for Megastars: Why Mammootty and Mohanlal still define Malayalam cinema Forty-plus years in business, Mammootty, 74, and Mohanlal, 65, remain the first names of Malayalam cinema. They've reunited in Patriot after 18 years and the film has opened big. Understanding how the two veterans stay relevant and dominant.

Rajpal Yadav loves Michael Jackson biopic, there's a Charlie Chaplin twist to it Comedian Rajpal Yadav praised Michael, the Michael Jackson biopic, calling it a "commendable tribute". There's a Charlie Chaplin connection to why Yadav loves the MJ film. Read here.

Lyricist-writer Prasoon Joshi appointed Prasar Bharati Chairman Prasoon Joshi has been appointed Chairman of Prasar Bharati. The lyricist-writer brings experience from films, advertising and his role as CBFC (Central Board of Film Certification) chief.

Rishab Shetty meets Amit Shah, praises his understanding of culture, cinema Rishab Shetty described his meeting with Amit Shah as memorable, highlighting their discussion on cinema, culture and heritage. The actor also shared that the Home Minister appreciated Kantara and spoke about his upcoming projects.

Is Tara Sutaria dating Aditya Roy Kapur after Veer Pahariya split rumours? Is Tara Sutaria dating Aditya Roy Kapur? A new report suggests so, even as speculation continues around her reported split with Veer Pahariya.

general news.headline: The big verdict: Bengal, TN, Kerala, Assam, Puducherry wait with bated breathCounting on May 4 will decide the contests in West Bengal, Tamil Nadu, Assam, Kerala and Puducherry. The results come amid a repoll row in Bengal, tight security and rival claims over the political mood.

Untenable claim: India after Nepal objects to Mansarovar Yatra via LipulekhNepal has formally objected to a key pilgrimage route via Lipulekh, sending a protest to India and China. The move revives a long-standing border dispute, even as Kathmandu calls for dialogue.

Will Shashi Tharoor be Kerala CM? Congress MP's 'clear answer' ahead of countingAs Kerala awaits election results, Shashi Tharoor faces the big question on the chief minister's post. His response shifts focus to Congress' internal process, even as UDF eyes a comeback.

Canada intel labels Khalistanis as security threat, links them to India violenceThe assessment, published in the Canadian Security Intelligence Service's 2025 public report, lays out concerns over fundraising, influence networks and links to extremist activity tied primarily to India.

Diljit stops Canada show, confronts pro-Khalistanis: Jinne jhande dikhane...Diljit Dosanjh paused his concert in Calgary, Canada, to address pro-Khalistani protesters waving flags during his show. The singer asked those disrupting the show to leave the act aside.

Iron grills, smart lock, nets: How Delhi building became a death trap during fireA fast-moving fire tore through a residential building in Delhi, killing nine people and injuring four. The combination of structural design flaws and safety measures that failed under stress left residents with almost no escape.

Election of rejection: Bengal BJP chief targets TMC day ahead of resultsState BJP president Samik Bhattacharya, speaking to IndiaToday.in ahead of the Assembly results, called it an "election of rejection" of Mamata Banerjee. He projected 200-plus seats and framed the campaign around Bengali pride, identity, and a "Bengal first" sentiment.

Impossible to live: Delhi judge's last call to family after wife insults his fatherDelhi judge Aman Sharma died by suicide after allegedly being unable to bear mental distress triggered by his wife's insult of his father, his family claimed. They have demanded a fair probe, alleging sustained harassment and escalating marital disputes led to the tragic incident.

Only 3% in IITs, IIMs, NITs but they take 50% of India's higher education budgetHandcrafted ByOur Editors A tiny elite in IITs, IIMs and NITs commands nearly half of India's higher education budget. The remaining 96% of students are left to navigate a vastly underfunded system spread across hundreds of institutions. This isn't just unequal spending, it's a pipeline that decides whose futures get priority.

Sealed envelope with phone seized outside Asansol strongroom, BJP cries foulOn the eve of the counting of votes in Bengal, a sealed envelope with documents and a switched-off phone was found in a vehicle on poll duty near the strongroom at Asansol Engineering College. The recovery triggered protests and a police probe.

Godrej Industries lays out 2031 growth ambitionsThe purpose statement, 'Crafting tomorrow since 1897', celebrates the Godrej Group's legacy while clearly positioning the new, refreshed Godrej Industries for the future.

Mittal family and Adar Poonawalla buy Rajasthan Royals in Rs 15,660 crore dealContrary to past reports, Steel giants the Mittal family and billionaire Adar Poonawalla have acquired Rajasthan Royals for Rs 15,660 crore. The sale of the champions of the inaugural edition of the IPL comes on the heels of the record-breaking sale of Royal Challengers Bengaluru.

Men rip off train seat covers for fun. Viral video sparks outrageA viral video showed a man ripping a train bench seat as another filmed him inside a moving coach. The clip drew angry reactions online.

India's AC dilemma: How it cools the heat and heats the planetThere lies a painful paradox at the heart of India's cooling crisis. The very machines helping people survive extreme heat also contribute to the warming, making that heat worse.

Vijay asks TVK winners to gather at resort after results amid poaching fearsAmid strong exit poll projections, Vijay has told winning TVK candidates to reach Panaiyur as soon as results are declared. The move reflects the party's effort to keep its elected members together after a candidate went missing during scrutiny.

Zero electricity bill, credit for selling surplus: Neighbour's envy driving solar in IndiaHouseholds across India are turning to rooftop solar through an unusual mix of triggers. From Varanasi to Greater Noida to a village in Maharashtra, neighbours are inspiring each other to go solar. Backed by government subsidies, growing awareness, and most importantly, lower electricity bills, solar is now powering more middle-class households in India than ever.

The trial that could kill OpenAIHandcrafted ByOur Editors Elon Musk and Sam Altman are fighting in court over OpenAI's shift to a for-profit structure. The case could reshape control of the company and test whether its duty to humanity was enforceable.
Question Time: Weekly News QuizThink you read the headlines this week? Let's test that!

UP teens climb water tank to shoot reel; 1 falls to death, 2 rescued by Air ForceA social media stunt on a 60-ft water tank in Uttar Pradesh turned fatal, leaving one child dead and two injured. Hours later, a dramatic overnight rescue operation was launched to save those still trapped.

Man sets off security alarms at Trump golf club days after DC shooting; arrestedUS Secret Service agents arrested a man after he became disruptive at a screening area at Trump Doral National Golf Club. The arrest came days after a major security breach at the White House Correspondents' Dinner.
What made Delhiites spend thousands on a Diet Coke party?Handcrafted ByOur Editors A Diet Coke party at a happening club? Well..Delhi showed up, and how.

Wheat grain cheaper now, but your atta, bread prices haven't dipped. Why?While wheat prices have gone down in mandis, there is no resultant relief in atta and bread prices for households. What's behind the mismatch between the rates at which farmers sell wheat and the retail prices of flour? Who is really benefiting?

Ranveer Allahbadia takes cheeky dig at past row after Kapil Sharma show. See postRanveer Allahbadia grabbed attention with his post after The Kapil show appearance, taking a cheeky dig at India's Got Latent row while also dedicating the moment to his late grandfather.

Forget Rs 40 crore retirement fund, Indian students can't even save Rs 3 croreHandcrafted ByOur Editors India's Rs 40 crore retirement estimate by Sandeep Jethwani may be mathematically sound, but salary data tells a different story. With most graduates earning modest incomes and job stability still a concern, reports from PLFS, Unstop, and income surveys reveal a widening gap between what Indians earn and what they need to retire.

sports.headline: KKR players smash cake on Andre Russell's face after win vs SRH In perhaps the most hilarious match-winning celebration in the Indian Premier League this season, KKR's player turned coach Andre Russell had cake smashed all over his face after the team beat SunRisers Hyderabad. KKR beat 3rd placed SRH in Hyderabad on Sunday.

GT survive PBKS' last over scare, tame chase drags into tense Ahmedabad finish GT vs PBKS, IPL 2026: Gujarat Titans survived a tense final-over scare to beat Punjab Kings by four wickets in Ahmedabad, with Suryansh Shedge's rescue act and Punjab's late fight turning 163 into a gripping thriller.

Is this peak IPL? Mark Wood reveals bizarre story about team owner England pacer Mark Wood narrated a bizarre story from his IPL stint, revealing how a franchise owner once offered to fly him in on a private plane to play a match in the tournament. Can you guess what IPL team and owner this could have been?

India will get the best out of Nandni Sharma: Jemimah Rodrigues backs young pacer Jemimah Rodrigues backed young fast bowler Nandni Sharma to deliver for India in the Women's T20 World Cup later this year after she earned her maiden national call-up for the global event.

Manchester United vs Liverpool: Unconscious Alex Ferguson taken to hospital Former Manchester United manager Sir Alex Ferguson was taken to hospital after feeling unwell ahead of the team's Premier League clash against Liverpool on Sunday. Ferguson had fallen unconscious ahead of the game at Old Trafford.

Jannik Sinner boosts French Open hopes after historic Madrid Open title run Madrid Open: World No.1 Jannik Sinner clinched the title after defeating Alexander Zverev 6-1, 6-2 in the final in just 57 minutes. He will now aim to complete a Career Golden Masters when he competes at the Italian Open.

Who is Suryansh Shedge? Punjab Kings youngster steps-up vs GT in Ahmedabad IPL 2026: Suryansh Shedge announced his arrival in the Indian Premier League with a fighting fifty for Punjab Kings against Gujarat Titans. Coming into bat with his side in crisis, Shedge's counter-attacking knock helped revive the innings in Ahmedabad.

Ramakrishna Ghosh ruled out of IPL 2026, sustains foot fracture after IPL debut IPL 2026: CSK's injury troubles worsened as Ramakrishna Ghosh was ruled out of IPL 2026 with a foot fracture, ending the uncapped all-rounder's campaign just after his debut

Shubman, getting married? Danny Morrison's question leaves GT captain irritated IPL 2026: Shubman Gill was left visibly irritated after commentator Danny Morrison asked him a personal question at the toss ahead of Gujarat Titans' clash against Punjab Kings. Here's what happened at the toss in Ahmedabad.

Rishabh Pant bogged down by price tag? LSG bowling coach addresses batter's poor form IPL 2026, MI vs LSG: Bharat Arun said the price tag has nothing to do with Rishabh Pant's indifferent run of form. The LSG bowling coach backed the captain to bounce back despite his modest returns this season.

India must tackle doping crisis to host Olympics: International Testing Agency India must urgently reform its anti-doping system to strengthen its 2036 Olympics bid, with ITA chief Benjamin Cohen warning that testing lapses and weak governance remain major concerns.

Spin twins Varun and Narine halt SRH juggernaut in Hyderabad as KKR hang onIPL 2026: Varun Chakravarthy and Sunil Narine spun SunRisers Hyderabad into submission, restricting them to 165 before Angkrish Raghuvanshi and Ajinkya Rahane completed a controlled chase. Three wins from three. KKR's unlikely revival has a heartbeat now.

GT vs PBKS Highlights: Gujarat Titans edge past Punjab Kings in last-over thrillerGT (Gujarat) vs PBKS (Punjab) Highlights, IPL 2026: Gujarat Titans beat the Punjab Kings by four wickets at the Narendra Modi Stadium in Ahmedabad. Chasing 164, the Titans got home with one ball to spare.

PV Sindhu joins Kaavya Maran to cheer for SunRisers in HyderabadSRH vs KKR: One of India's top badminton players, PV Sindhu, attended the Indian Premier League match between SunRisers Hyderabad and Kolkata Knight Riders. Sindhu hung out with SRH owner Kaviya Maran at the Rajiv Gandhi International Stadium.

200 for Sunil Narine: KKR great becomes first overseas bowler to hit double tonIPL 2026: Sunil Narine became only the third bowler to go past 200 wickets in the Indian Premier League. The West Indies great took two wickets in a disciplined bowling effort from Kolkata Knight Riders in Hyderabad on Sunday, May 3.

Jemimah Rodrigues says India hungry to keep title run going with T20 World CupJemimah Rodrigues said India are hungrier than ever for a second world title, with the team targeting T20 World Cup success in England after last year's landmark ODI triumph.

Manish Pandey gets a game in IPL 2026, joins Rohit and Virat in elite listIPL 2026: Kolkata Knight Riders recalled Manish Pandey for the SunRisers Hyderabad match, replacing Ramandeep Singh. The appearance placed him alongside Virat Kohli and Rohit Sharma as players to feature in all 19 IPL seasons.

IPL throwback: How Ashwin got MS Dhoni to lose his cool, earn his big break at CSKR Ashwin recalled how dismissing MS Dhoni in the Challenger Trophy and celebrating the wicket earned attention, helped him break into CSK, and began a long cricketing bond.

Mittal family and Adar Poonawalla buy Rajasthan Royals in Rs 15,660 crore dealContrary to past reports, Steel giants the Mittal family and billionaire Adar Poonawalla have acquired Rajasthan Royals for Rs 15,660 crore. The sale of the champions of the inaugural edition of the IPL comes on the heels of the record-breaking sale of Royal Challengers Bengaluru.

I was harassed by Brij Bhushan, I am one of the six victims: Vinesh PhogatOlympian Vinesh Phogat, a key face of the 2023 New Delhi protests, has identified herself as one of the six women harassed by Brij Bhushan Sharan Singh, alleging upcoming trials for Asian Games 2026 are rigged to block her comeback.

Hardik is a plastic captain: Ex-selector slams MI skipper's intent in CSK lossKris Srikkanth tore into Hardik Pandya after Mumbai Indians' outing against Chennai Super Kings at Chepauk. The criticism centred on Pandya's 18 off 23 balls and CSK's method of tying him down through the middle overs.
The Vibhuti Effect: Why do India's chess prodigies swear by the sacred ash?Why India's chess prodigies carry an ancient ritual into the world's most cerebral arena, and what it's doing to their game.

Has Hardik lost MI dressing room?EX-IND batter's big claim after CSK defeatManoj Tiwary said Hardik Pandya appears to have lost the Mumbai Indians dressing room after the defeat to Chennai Super Kings. He said the side looked fundamentally unsettled, with limited backing for the captain and inconsistent performances damaging the campaign.

No longer CSK's Rs. 14.2 Crore gamble, Kartik Sharma spreads his wings in yellowKartik Sharma finally proved that he wasn't just a big-money gamble for CSK, repaying the faith of the management by scoring a well-composed fifty to get his side the win in the El Clasico against Mumbai.

IPL 2026: Punjab's bowling in focus as Gujarat look to spice up playoffs raceThe IPL 2026 playoff race is heating up as Gujarat Titans look to keep their momentum going against the in-form Punjab Kings. Sunrisers Hyderabad will aim to edge closer to a spot in the playoffs as they come up against a resurgent KKR side.

From T20 World Cup heroes to IPL 2026 flops: Will MI rest their Indian core?Mahela Jayawardene has defended Mumbai Indians' Indian core after another defeat deepened their poor IPL 2026 run. With Bumrah, Hardik Pandya, Tilak Varma and Suryakumar Yadav struggling, MI are clinging to a mathematical chance of qualification.

IPL Play of the Day: Overton becomes death overs ace, leaves MI high and dryIPL 2026, CSK vs MI: From having very little experience in bowling at the death, Jamie Overton has evolved significantly, and that improvement was evident when he choked the Mumbai Indians batters in the El Clasico on Saturday at the MA Chidambaram Stadium.

Mumbai Indians can still qualify for the IPL 2026 playoffs. Here's howIPL 2026, CSK vs MI: Hardik Pandya's Mumbai Indians can still make their way through to the playoffs despite their heavy eight-wicket defeat to the Chennai Super Kings on Saturday at the MA Chidambaram Stadium.

No Lakshya. No party: India blown away by France 0-3 in semi-finalsThomas Cup: India were blown away by France in the semi-finals of Thomas Cup on Saturday, May 2. With Lakshya Sen forced to sit out of the tie, India lost 0-3 in straight games against the French.

Kartik Sharma recreates MS Dhoni's gun celebration after maiden IPL fifty vs MIKartik Sharma's pre-planned gun celebration after his maiden IPL fifty against Mumbai Indians sent Chepauk into a Dhoni flashback on Saturday night.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
